rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MURPHY et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 24, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT CANNOT RELY ON ITS INTERROGATORY RESPONSES TO ESTABLISH A FACT AND PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MURPHY et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 24, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO ROBERTS EXHIBIT B IS SUSTAINED AS HEARSAY. DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A FOUNDATION THAT THE COMPUTER RECORD IS A BUSINESS RECORD. EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1552 OPERATES ONLY TO ESTABLISH THAT A COMPUTER'S PRINT FUNCTION HAS WORKED. IT DOES NOT CREATE A HEARSAY EXCEPTION. PEOPLE V HAWKINS (2002) 98 CAL APP 4TH 1428, 1449-50. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LISA FOX VS. TANE CHAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 24, 2007, LINE 5. DEFENDANT SUPERIOR CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE ALLEGED DEFECT WAS PATENT OR LATENT. THE PLACEMENT OF THE SWITCH WAS OBVIOUS; ITS OPERATION WAS NOT BASED ON THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE 1 AND 2 ARE SUSTAINED, 3 THROUGH 8 ARE OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/AP)
